meta.title: About
meta.description: Louis Seo — a system-oriented product designer building clarity into multi-role platforms, AI products, and design systems.

hero.label: About
hero.descLine1: For complex products, structure comes before the screen.
hero.descLine2: I shape systems tangled with roles, data, and workflows into experiences that are clearer and built to scale.

whatIBring.title: What I Bring
whatIBring.desc: I read structure before industry. My work is about taking products with messy role, data, and workflow problems and resolving them into clear experiences.

cards.title: Product System Design
cards.text: Built a 70+ component design system from scratch and rolled it out to four product teams. I have run the full lifecycle — token architecture, Figma library operations, documentation, and team adoption — myself.

cards.title: AI & Data Experience
cards.text: Designed an AI defect detection app on deep learning, a wind turbine risk prediction dashboard, and a real-time sensor monitoring system. My focus: make model output something users can trust and act on.

cards.title: Platform & Multi-Role UX
cards.text: Shipped a B2B property management system, a B2C resident app, and an investment analytics dashboard in parallel — handling how the same data gets used differently across roles. I'm comfortable designing role-specific workflows on platforms where B2B, B2C, and B2G coexist.

cards.title: Design–Engineering Bridge
cards.text: Established a company-wide UX guideline and stood up a design QA process I ran myself. I understand component specs, state definitions, and React patterns well enough to make sure intent survives the handoff from Figma to code.

items.desc: Global SaaS platform for real estate rental and investment. I lead UX across four products in parallel: B2B PMS, B2C resident app and web, admin back office, and the Investment Manager analytics platform. Drove a new e-contract product from zero to one. Redesigned the legacy PMS around operational workflows. Across operators, residents, admins, and analysts, I design the information architecture and role-specific workflows that let the same data work in different contexts.

AI drone inspection platform company. Shipped 6+ products: B2B SaaS web, B2G drone operations and command systems for military and police (confidential), and a mobile autonomous flight app. Built the company-wide UX standard design system and established the solution identity. Introduced and ran the design QA process. Contributed to products awarded the iF Design Award (Service Design; AI & Robotics) and the Red Dot Design Award. Co-inventor on an employee invention patent for real-time information display and role-based workflow in mission-critical environments.

Australian PropTech SaaS startup. Designed three products in parallel: a resident management app used by strata managers, facility operators, and tenants (We Services); a hotel operations automation app (Wumbotel); and a remote healthcare service (Wumbo Care). Owned the full lifecycle from feature planning to design, launch, and ongoing operations. Collaborated in English with a multinational team across B2B operator and B2C user workflows.

Australian IoT startup. Designed the UX for a smart shower mobile app and developed brand identity, value, and BX concepts in parallel. My first time treating hardware product, digital interface, and brand as a single design surface.

items.name: Employee Invention Patent
items.meta: Method and apparatus for displaying information provided by an aircraft · 2023

principles.title: Start with the domain and the data flow
principles.text: Before I draw a screen, I map the business logic, the data model, and the relationships between user roles. Whether it's a wind farm inspection workflow or real estate investment reporting, the right structure doesn't come without understanding the domain first.

principles.title: Build the system before the screens
principles.text: Instead of designing pages one at a time, I work in tokens, components, and patterns. I've run multiple products on a shared design system that still works in each product's own context.

principles.title: Design at a level that ships
principles.text: API response shapes, component states, rendering constraints — I bake these into the design from the start. Engineers shouldn't have to interpret design intent. Running design QA myself has narrowed the gap between intent and the final result.

principles.title: Ship fast, validate in real use
principles.text: A working prototype beats a perfect spec. Field engineers tapping a screen with gloves on. Investors comparing hundreds of assets on a single view. The feedback that finishes the design comes from real conditions.

whatsNext.title: What's Next
whatsNext.intro: I want to keep solving structural complexity — on products with more users and bigger reach.
